title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Runtime Lens: Memory Budget
summary: A large-file mode still calls read_text, splits tokens at chunk edges, decodes partial UTF-8 incorrectly, retains every token, and claims bounded memory without measurement. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the memory budget increment in bounded streaming analyzer. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Set byte, vocabulary, token-fragment, line, output, and elapsed-time budgets with rejection behavior.
Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_streaming_bounded_memory_2(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Build Memory Budget evidence · Theory Memory Budget
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-memory-budget-17i4ug
# Competency: bookbot-memory-budget.
# Case 3876: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_memory_budget_17i4ug(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17i4ug = "17i4ug-gu4i71-3876"
    assert evidence_variant_17i4ug
    evidence_axes_17i4ug = (
        "corpus17i4ug",
        "policygu4i71",
        "revision3876",
        "command17i4ug3876",
        "reportgu4i713876",
        "test387617i4ug",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17i4ug)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set byte, vocabulary, token-fragment, line, output, and elapsed-time budgets with rejection behavior.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Memory Budget model · Theory Memory Budget, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Streaming eliminates every possible memory-growth path.
